topic: Learning curves in AI use and skill-biased adoption
goal: Explore how tenure with general-purpose AI changes task choice, model selection, collaboration habits, success rates, and inequality of outcomes.
seed_questions: How much of expert-looking AI use is learned behavior versus early-adopter selection and survivorship bias?
Which usage habits actually cause higher success with AI rather than merely correlating with it?

seed_definitions.name: learning by doing
seed_definitions.meaning: Improvement in AI-assisted performance that comes from repeated use, experimentation, and adaptation rather than static user traits.

seed_definitions.name: tenure effect
seed_definitions.meaning: A systematic difference in behavior or outcomes associated with how long a user has been working with an AI system.
